behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in real STT model behavior (speaker diarization/label preservation). A stubbed transcript would hide dropped or incorrect speaker labels. System was considered but rejected because the behavior text targets label correctness, not transport/network mechanics. Workflow was considered but rejected because the behavior text is specifically about STT output preservation, not validating a multi-step agent pipeline outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test with real meeting audio against the real STT provider and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for known speakers (enough for downstream attendee attribution).

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/mapping logic on transcript text and schema structure. The failure boundary is local code, not OS/network/model output. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require cross-module I/O or external dependency behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser with transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments and assert extracted records include correct assignee and task text fields in the summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summarization decisions (coverage of assignees in action items). Deterministic mocks would mask misses. Workflow was considered but rejected because this behavior can be validated from transcript-to-summary in a single real model step; the text does not require verifying multi-agentic stage chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with known assigned attendees to the real summarization model and assert output action items cover each attendee at least once.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified. The bug boundary is local transformation into schema fields. Higher levels were considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly scopes to routing logic, not model quality or external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the routing function an input containing pre-identified decisions and narrative text, then assert decision entries land only in `decisions` schema fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This requires real model judgment to detect and place key decisions correctly in structured output. Stubbed responses would always pass and hide extraction/placement quality failures. Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text is about transcript-to-live-summary decision placement, which can be tested as a single model-output quality boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts with known decision statements and validate real LLM output includes those decisions in `decisions` (not only narrative text).

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly chains multiple processing steps across segments and requires coherent merged output, which depends on repeated model calls and handoff/merge across steps. Lower levels miss multi-step degradation and cross-segment consistency failures. Agentic was considered but rejected because the text requires sequential multi-step processing and merged coherence across segments, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end on a >60-minute real recording; verify segmentation and sequential processing occurred, and final merged summary is structurally complete and consistent across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is explicitly full-pipeline with real input and multiple agentic stages (STT then LLM summarization) plus delivery. Real end-to-end orchestration and model-dependent steps must both succeed without intervention. System/Agentic alone are too narrow because they do not cover the full multi-step pipeline boundary stated in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real meeting audio file from upload through structured output delivery, asserting successful completion, schema-valid output, and no manual retries or operator actions.
